summary: Rebased ws-0295 onto latest origin/main, discovered and fixed a Docker publication helper path regression in the current mainline live-apply path, reran the governed build-artifact-cache replay on docker-build-lv3, and re-verified the cache plane with the refreshed 50-image seed plan, lv3-buildkitd/buildx, apt-cacher-ng, and nftables forward compatibility.

verification.check: Repo validation and targeted regression coverage on the rebased replay branch
verification.observed: `./scripts/validate_repo.sh workstream-surfaces data-models` passed before the workstream returned to its terminal `live_applied` state; `./scripts/validate_repo.sh generated-docs` intentionally exposed stale canonical truth while the workstream was marked `in_progress`; `uvx --from pyyaml python scripts/canonical_truth.py --write` refreshed `README.md` and unblocked the governed wrapper; after rebasing to latest `origin/main`, `uv run --with pytest python -m pytest -q tests/test_artifact_cache_seed.py tests/test_artifact_cache_runtime_role.py tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py tests/test_service_id_resolver.py tests/test_docker_publication_assurance_playbook.py` returned `24 passed in 0.45s`; and the final `generated-docs`, `agent-standards`, and standalone `data-models` reruns all passed again.

verification.check: Scoped build-artifact-cache automation path on the fresh and rebased replay worktrees
verification.observed: `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/ansible_scope_runner.py run --inventory inventory/hosts.yml --run-id ws0295syntax-r2 --playbook playbooks/services/build-artifact-cache.yml --env production -- --private-key .local/ssh/hetzner_llm_agents_ed25519 -e proxmox_guest_ssh_connection_mode=proxmox_host_jump --syntax-check` passed before the first replay, and the same scope passed again from the rebased latest-main worktree as `ws0295syntax-r4`.

Direct SSH verification through the Proxmox jump path confirmed `PORT:5001=ok`, `PORT:5002=ok`, `PORT:5003=ok`, and `PORT:5004=ok`; all four `artifact-cache-*` containers were already up; `systemctl is-active lv3-buildkitd` returned `active`; `docker buildx inspect lv3-cache --bootstrap` succeeded; `apt-cacher-ng` served its local report page; but `jq '.seed_images | length' /opt/artifact-cache/seed-plan.json` still returned the older `41`-image warm set.

verification.check: Latest-main Docker publication helper staging repair
verification.observed: After rebasing onto `origin/main` commit `9ce18e9c35cd4c8e97411c92e6dada5c3a2c3dd7`, the first exact-main rerun failed in `lv3.platform.docker_runtime : Install the Docker publication assurance helper` because `playbook_dir ~ '/../scripts/docker_publication_assurance.py'` resolved to a nonexistent `playbooks/scripts/` path. This branch repaired the lookup to `playbook_dir ~ '/../../scripts/docker_publication_assurance.py'` in `collections/ansible_collections/lv3/platform/roles/docker_runtime/tasks/main.yml` and updated `tests/test_docker_runtime_role.py` to match.

verification.check: Exact-main governed build-artifact-cache replay after the helper-path fix
verification.observed: `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true make live-apply-service service=build-artifact-cache env=production EXTRA_ARGS='-e bypass_promotion=true'` succeeded from the rebased latest-main worktree rooted on `9ce18e9c35cd4c8e97411c92e6dada5c3a2c3dd7`, recorded the expected promotion-bypass event and ADR 0191 override, and finished with `docker-build-lv3 : ok=119 changed=4 unreachable=0 failed=0 skipped=11 rescued=0 ignored=0`.

verification.check: Post-apply cache-plane and Docker bridge verification
verification.observed: Final post-apply SSH verification confirmed `PORT:5001=ok`, `PORT:5002=ok`, `PORT:5003=ok`, and `PORT:5004=ok`; the four `artifact-cache-*` containers stayed up alongside `lv3-buildkitd`; `/opt/artifact-cache/seed-plan.json` still reported `50` warmable images; `systemctl is-active lv3-buildkitd` returned `active`; `docker buildx inspect lv3-cache --bootstrap` still succeeded; `curl -fsS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:3142/acng-report.html` returned `200`; local `scripts/artifact_cache_seed.py` against the current repo catalogs also returned `50`; and `sudo nft list chain inet filter forward` still showed accepts for both `172.16.0.0/12` and `192.168.0.0/16`.

notes: The cache plane itself remained healthy between live applies, but the guest-side seed plan had drifted behind the current repo catalogs at 41 warmable images before the March 30 replay. The replay refreshed that drift back to the current 50-image set.

The replay intentionally used `ALLOW_IN_PLACE_MUTATION=true` as the documented ADR 0191 narrow exception for `docker-build-lv3`; immutable guest replacement remains the preferred steady-state delivery path until ADR 0296 moves the cache plane to a dedicated guest.

Rebasing onto latest `origin/main` exposed a real mainline regression from `dc3387ad0` in the Docker publication assurance helper lookup path. This workstream repaired that path and its regression test before re-running the exact-main live apply.

The shared live-apply wrapper also reasserted Docker bridge forward compatibility on `docker-build-lv3`, including the nftables accepts for `172.16.0.0/12` and `192.168.0.0/16` that the current Docker runtime expects.

The final branch still records `ws-0295-live-apply` as `live_applied`, which means `./scripts/validate_repo.sh workstream-surfaces` intentionally rejects the branch once the workstream is terminal. That validator had already passed earlier on the same branch before the terminal state was restored.

No `VERSION`, release `changelog.md`, or `versions/stack.yaml` bump was required on this workstream branch because ADR 0295 was already represented in canonical mainline truth; merge-to-main carries the latest-main automation repair, refreshed evidence, and the regenerated README canonical-truth table.
